cards.front: What is the primary goal of periodic noise reduction by frequency domain filtering?
cards.back: To remove periodic noise from an image by filtering out specific frequency components

cards.front: What type of noise is typically reduced using frequency domain filtering?
cards.back: Periodic noise, such as blocky or grid-like noise

cards.front: What is the main difference between spatial domain filtering and frequency domain filtering?
cards.back: Frequency domain filtering examines the image in the frequency domain, whereas spatial domain filtering examines the image in the spatial domain

cards.front: What is the purpose of the inverse discrete Fourier transform in frequency domain filtering?
cards.back: To convert the filtered frequency domain image back into the spatial domain

cards.front: What is the advantage of using frequency domain filtering for periodic noise reduction?
cards.back: It can effectively remove periodic noise, which can be challenging to remove using spatial domain filtering techniques
